name: kbridge-spherical-four-bar-animate
description: Fourth mechanism through the animate pipeline and the FIRST fully-3D one (every prior kbridge mechanism is planar): 0529_spherical_4_bar_linkage_mechanism_2b, picked fresh from the corpus (4 bodies, 4 revolutes whose axes all intersect at one point - the links move on a sphere; dyn-sim keyframes carry a full ~180 deg recorded cycle). Translated clean on the first pass (0 errors/warnings; assembly converges 0 iters at rms 2.4e-13 - the kf0-derived frame x-axes hold in full 3D with no new fixes). The bridge inferred NO driver (B-cand-004 family), so the Sweep node passes an explicit Joint override = joint_0 (Base<->CrankA), 0 -> 2.967 rad (170 deg; full 360 hits the ~180 deg dead-center, B-cand-008). Probed 35/35 both directions headlessly. Binds the 4 Inventor .3dm bodies (part-local mm, out/spherical-four-bar/bodies/), poses mid-sweep at Step 17/34. Pass/fail = Converged + SlopSuccess asserts. doc=MM, CPig-only. Slop: KinematicBridge/out/_slop-side-by-side/spherical-four-bar-bind-sweep-animate.canary.slop.json.

setup.vlmDescription: A spherical four-bar linkage shown as shaded solid CAD parts: a curved base, two curved crank links, and a curved connecting rod, all arcing over a common center so the links move on a sphere, posed mid-rotation, overlaid with a small CPig frame-triad skeleton. The parts form one connected closed-loop linkage at a sane scale.

checkpoints.name: persp
checkpoints.description: Inventor spherical 4-bar CAD parts posed mid-sweep by CPig's solve, on the CPig skeleton.

checkpoints.name: front
checkpoints.description: Front view of the CPig-posed spherical 4-bar parts.
